edges.description: Integrated molecular testing resolves most legacy mixed gliomas.
Molecularly classified diffuse gliomas remain infiltrating brain tumors.
IDH-mutant oncometabolism is a defining molecular context for current classification and targeted therapy.

nodes.description: Diffuse infiltrating glioma growth in the brain can present with headache, seizures, neurocognitive impairment, and focal neurologic deficits.

evidence.reference_title: Glioblastoma and Other Primary Brain Malignancies in Adults: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Symptoms of malignant brain tumors include headache (50%), seizures (20%-50%), neurocognitive impairment (30%-40%), and focal neurologic deficits (10%-40%).

nodes.description: Cancer-associated IDH mutations can confer neomorphic enzymatic activity that produces the oncometabolite 2-hydroxyglutarate. This provides a mechanism-level link between IDH-mutant diffuse glioma biology and mutant IDH inhibition by vorasidenib.

evidence.snippet: cancer-associated IDH1 mutations result in a new ability of the enzyme to catalyse the NADPH-dependent reduction of alpha-ketoglutarate to R(-)-2-hydroxyglutarate (2HG).

nodes.description: The legacy diagnosis is based on diffuse glioma histology with admixed oligodendroglial-like and astrocytic-like tumor morphology. Current classification retains histology as part of integrated diagnosis but treats molecular diagnostics as central for CNS tumor classification.

evidence.reference_title: The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System: a summary.
evidence.snippet: the 2021 fifth edition introduces major changes that advance the role of molecular diagnostics in CNS tumor classification.

nodes.description: Integrated diagnosis uses IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion to translate most historical oligoastrocytoma diagnoses into current adult-type diffuse glioma categories. IDH-mutant tumors without whole-arm 1p/19q codeletion are generally classified as astrocytoma, IDH-mutant, while IDH-mutant tumors with whole-arm 1p/19q codeletion are classified as oligodendroglioma.

evidence.reference_title: Epidemiology and survival of adult-type diffuse glioma in Belgium during the molecular era.
evidence.snippet: The gathered data were transformed into the 2021 World Health Organization classification of CNS tumors using the IDH- and 1p/19q-mutation status.

nodes.description: Vorasidenib is an oral brain-penetrant mutant IDH1/2 inhibitor that may delay progression and subsequent anticancer intervention in selected residual or recurrent grade 2 IDH-mutant glioma after surgery only. This is not specific to historical oligoastrocytoma but is relevant to the IDH-mutant diffuse gliomas that replaced the legacy category.

evidence.reference_title: Vorasidenib in IDH1- or IDH2-Mutant Low-Grade Glioma.
evidence.snippet: In patients with grade 2 IDH-mutant glioma, vorasidenib significantly improved progression-free survival and delayed the time to the next intervention.
